title: Remove Z-Axis Gearbox Access Components and Release Belt Drive for Gearbox Replacement
summary: Perform the documented removal-preparation steps for Z-axis gearbox replacement: manually position the A-axis and counter-balance, apply lockout/tagout, remove the station side panel, secure both carriages with M6 socket-head screws, remove both belt clamps, release belt tension, loosen the idler pulley assembly lock screws, and refer to the separate motor removal procedure if motor removal is required.
when_to_use: Use when performing the documented removal preparation for Z-axis gearbox replacement in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual section 7.3.7.1 Removal, including access opening and belt-drive release steps before gearbox removal proceeds.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete gearbox removal or re-assembly procedure.
Do not use this runbook for Z-axis motor removal details; use the separately referenced Motor Removal procedure on page 106 if motor removal is needed.
Do not use this runbook if the A-axis and counter-balance cannot be positioned between the threaded holes as described.
Do not use this runbook if carriage securing screws cannot be installed below both carriages before belt tension is released.

responsibilities: Perform HMI jog positioning of the A-axis and counter-balance.
Apply lockout/tagout to the tipper using the referenced operator station LOTO procedure.
Remove access hardware and side panel.
Secure both carriages before releasing belt tension.
Remove belt clamps and loosen tensioning and idler pulley lock screws.
Stop and escalate if safe support conditions for the carriages cannot be established.

safety_notes: This procedure includes lockout/tagout and mechanical work around belt tension and suspended axis components, so it is not operator-safe.
Secure both carriages with M6 socket-head screws before releasing belt tension to prevent the A-axis and counter-balance from dropping.
Most fasteners have Loctite 277 (red), so removal may be more difficult.
Motor removal details are not included in this section; use the separately referenced procedure if needed.
access_or_tools_needed: HMI access for manual jog positioning
M6 socket-head screws for carriage securing
Access to the station side panel, motor area, belt clamps, tensioning screws, and idler pulley assembly

access_required: HMI access for manual jog positioning
Physical access to the station side panel and gearbox drive area
system_preconditions: The A-axis and counter-balance can be manually positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
Both carriage support holes below the carriages are accessible for M6 socket-head screw installation.
The tipper can be placed in a lockout/tagout state before panel removal and belt-tension release.
external_references: Motor Removal procedure on page 106 if motor removal is required

visual_references.description: Gearbox maintenance figure referenced in the source section for gearbox removal preparation and access.
Timing belt figure showing related belt-drive positioning and carriage support context.
Idler pulley assembly figure for identifying the assembly and lock screw area.
Additional gearbox figure for identifying gearbox area and side-panel access context.
Counter-balance related figure for orientation to the counter-balance side of the mechanism.

steps.title: Position the A-axis and counter-balance between actuator base holes
steps.purpose: Place the moving components in the documented position so the carriages can be mechanically secured before belt tension is released.
steps.instruction: Use the HMI to manually position (jog) the A-axis and counter-balance between the threaded holes in the actuator base. Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis and counter-balance are positioned between the threaded holes adjacent to each rail.
steps.healthy_condition: Both carriages are visibly aligned between the adjacent threaded holes on their respective rails.
steps.failure_condition: The A-axis and counter-balance cannot be positioned as described, or the carriage positions do not align with the threaded holes needed for securing.

actions.label: Jog A-axis and counter-balance into position
actions.exact_action: Use the HMI to manually position (jog) the A-axis and counter-balance between the threaded holes in the actuator base.

actions.label: Verify carriage alignment to holes
actions.exact_action: Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gearbox area and actuator base context for the A-axis/counter-balance positioning step.
Positioning context showing the A-axis and counter-balance between threaded holes in the actuator base.

stop_or_escalate_if: The A-axis and counter-balance cannot be positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
The carriage positions do not align with the holes needed for securing.

source_evidence.text: Use the HMI to manually position (jog) the A-axis and counter-balance between the threaded holes in the actuator base. Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.
Removal steps near this figure include jogging the A-axis and counter-balance into position.
The removal steps instruct the technician to manually position the A-axis and counterbalance between threaded holes in the actuator base.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe maintenance state before opening access and releasing belt tension.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in the documented lockout/tagout state for maintenance.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper is locked out and tagged out per the referenced procedure before mechanical access continues.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO cannot be completed or verified.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: LOTO cannot be completed or verified before panel removal or belt-tension release.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel
steps.purpose: Open access to the motor and gearbox drive area.
steps.instruction: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed and the motor access area is open.
steps.healthy_condition: All nine self-tapping fasteners are removed and the side panel is off without blocking access to the motor area.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed, fasteners cannot be removed, or access to the motor area remains obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove station side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gearbox and motor access area associated with side-panel removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed safely.
Fasteners cannot be removed with the documented tool.
Motor access is still obstructed after panel removal.

source_evidence.text: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
The removal procedure instructs the technician to LOTO the tipper, remove nine self-tapping screws from the side panel if needed to access the motor.
Removal steps near this figure include removing nine self-tapping fasteners from the side panel.

steps.title: Secure both carriages with M6 socket-head screws
steps.purpose: Prevent the A-axis and counter-balance from dropping when belt tension is released.
steps.instruction: Insert an M6 socket-head screw into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counter-balance from dropping once belt tension is released.
steps.expected_result: Both carriages are mechanically supported by installed M6 socket-head screws below them.
steps.healthy_condition: An M6 socket-head screw is installed below each carriage before any belt-tension release occurs.
steps.failure_condition: One or both carriage securing screws cannot be installed, or the carriages are not positively supported before belt tension is released.

actions.label: Install carriage support screws
actions.exact_action: Insert an M6 socket-head screw into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counter-balance from dropping once belt tension is released.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Carriage area and support-hole context below both carriages.
Support-hole and carriage securing context used before releasing belt tension.

stop_or_escalate_if: The carriage securing screws cannot be installed below both carriages before belt tension is released.
Either carriage is not positively supported.

source_evidence.text: Insert an M6 socket-head screw into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counter-balance from dropping once belt tension is released.
Removal steps near this figure include inserting M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.
The removal steps instruct the technician to install M6 socket-head screws below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping when belt tension is released.

steps.title: Remove the A-axis and counter-balance belt clamps
steps.purpose: Disconnect both carriages from the belt before tension is released further.
steps.instruction: Remove the A-axis belt clamp and the counter-balance belt clamp by removing the six M6 screws in each clamp. Verify both carriages are free from the belt after the clamps are removed.
steps.expected_result: Both belt clamps are removed and both carriages are free from the belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The A-axis belt clamp and counter-balance belt clamp are removed, and both carriages are no longer attached to the belt.
steps.failure_condition: One or both clamps cannot be removed, or one or both carriages remain attached to the belt after clamp removal.

actions.label: Remove A-axis belt clamp
actions.exact_action: Remove the A-axis belt clamp by removing the six M6 screws in the clamp.

actions.label: Remove counter-balance belt clamp
actions.exact_action: Remove the counter-balance belt clamp by removing the six M6 screws in the clamp.

actions.label: Verify both carriages are free from the belt
actions.exact_action: Verify both carriages are free from the belt after the clamps are removed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gearbox drive area where the belt clamps are accessed.
Timing belt path and clamp-related belt connection context.

stop_or_escalate_if: One or both belt clamps cannot be removed.
Both carriages are not free from the belt after clamp removal.

source_evidence.text: Remove the A-axis belt clamp and the counter-balance belt clamp by removing the six M6 screws in each clamp. Verify both carriages are free from the belt after the clamps are removed.

steps.title: Release belt tension at the lower end
steps.purpose: Reduce belt tension so the gearbox removal can proceed.
steps.instruction: Release belt tension by loosening the two M6x35 socket-head tensioning screws on the lower end.
steps.expected_result: The belt tension is released at the lower end.
steps.healthy_condition: The two lower-end M6x35 socket-head tensioning screws are loosened and belt tension is relieved.
steps.failure_condition: The tensioning screws cannot be loosened or belt tension is not released as expected.

actions.label: Loosen lower-end tensioning screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the two M6x35 socket-head tensioning screws on the lower end to release belt tension.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt and lower-end tensioning area associated with belt tension release.
Gearbox-side belt-drive context for the tension-release step.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tensioning screws cannot be loosened.
Belt tension does not release as expected.
Either carriage support condition changes after tension is reduced.

source_evidence.text: Release belt tension by loosening the two M6x35 socket-head tensioning screws on the lower end.

steps.title: Loosen the idler pulley assembly lock screws
steps.purpose: Release the idler pulley assembly from its locked position as part of gearbox removal preparation.
steps.instruction: Loosen the two M8 socket-head screws that lock the idler pulley assembly in place.
steps.expected_result: The idler pulley assembly lock screws are loosened.
steps.healthy_condition: Both M8 socket-head screws locking the idler pulley assembly are loosened.
steps.failure_condition: The idler pulley assembly lock screws cannot be loosened or the assembly remains locked in place.

actions.label: Loosen idler pulley assembly lock screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the two M8 socket-head screws that lock the idler pulley assembly in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly and the lock-screw area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The two M8 socket-head screws cannot be loosened.
The idler pulley assembly remains locked in place after the screws are loosened.

source_evidence.text: Loosen the two M8 socket-head screws that lock the idler pulley assembly in place.

Use this artifact when searching for idler pulley assembly identification, pulley replacement, belt tension release preparation, A-axis support, counterbalance securing, or tipper maintenance visuals.

steps.title: Refer to the separate motor removal procedure if needed
steps.purpose: Prevent unsupported motor-removal work from being improvised from this partial section.
steps.instruction: If needed, remove the Z-axis motor using the separately referenced Motor Removal procedure on page 106.
steps.expected_result: Motor removal, if required, is handed off to the correct referenced procedure rather than improvised from this section.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician uses the separate motor removal procedure when motor removal is required.
steps.failure_condition: Motor removal is attempted using undocumented steps from this section alone.

actions.label: Determine whether motor removal is needed
actions.exact_action: If needed, remove the Z-axis motor using the separately referenced Motor Removal procedure on page 106.

stop_or_escalate_if: Motor removal is required but the separate Motor Removal procedure is not available.
There is pressure to invent motor-removal steps from this section alone.

source_evidence.text: If needed, remove the Z-axis motor using the separately referenced Motor Removal procedure on page 106.
Use the separately referenced Motor Removal procedure for motor removal details; do not invent missing steps from this section alone.

success_criteria: The A-axis and counter-balance are positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
The tipper is locked out and tagged out.
The station side panel is removed for motor/gearbox access.
Both carriages are secured with M6 socket-head screws below them before belt tension is released.
The A-axis belt clamp and counter-balance belt clamp are removed.
Both carriages are free from the belt.
The lower-end M6x35 tensioning screws are loosened to release belt tension.
The two M8 socket-head screws locking the idler pulley assembly are loosened.
Any required motor removal is deferred to the separate referenced procedure.
healthy_conditions: Carriages align with the threaded-hole positions described in the actuator base.
LOTO is completed before mechanical access and belt work.
Side panel removal provides access to the motor area.
Both carriages are positively supported before belt tension is released.
Both carriages are free from the belt after clamp removal.
Belt tension is relieved and the idler pulley assembly lock screws are loosened.
failure_conditions: The A-axis and counter-balance cannot be positioned as described.
LOTO cannot be completed or verified.
The side panel cannot be removed.
Carriage securing screws cannot be installed below both carriages.
One or both belt clamps cannot be removed.
Both carriages are not free from the belt after clamp removal.
The lower-end tensioning screws cannot be loosened or belt tension is not released.
The idler pulley assembly lock screws cannot be loosened.
Motor removal is attempted without the separate referenced procedure.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the A-axis and counter-balance cannot be positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
Stop and escalate if carriage securing screws cannot be installed below both carriages before belt tension is released.
Stop and escalate if LOTO cannot be completed or verified.
Escalate to the separately referenced Motor Removal procedure on page 106 if motor removal is required.
Do not invent missing gearbox-removal or motor-removal steps not supported by this source section.
